contaminant.name: Mineral Scale / Hard Water Deposits
contaminant.description: After prolonged heat exposure in water-prone areas, scale-buildup forms as adherent oxide layers through oxidation process. This contamination exhibits tenacious bonding to surfaces and rough porous structure, so it differs from loose particulates or oily residues that detach easily. On reactive metals like steel, thick coatings insulate and promote corrosion underneath, while on inert non-metals such as alumina, thinner layers invite less cracking; removal challenges strong adhesion, so laser cleaning ablates precisely without substrate damage.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Carbon Dioxide generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
